old_summary: AbbVie in collaboration with Xilio therapeutics are developing a novel tumor-activated immunotherapies for the treatment of cancer (Press release, AbbVie, 12 Feb 2025, https://news.abbvie.com/2025-02-12-AbbVie-and-Xilio-Therapeutics-Announce-Collaboration-and-Option-Agreement-to-Develop-Novel-Tumor-Activated-Immunotherapies).
new_summary: ABBV-514, developed by AbbVie, is a CCR8 specific afucosylated antibody that targets and eliminates immunosuppressive tumor regulatory T cells, enhancing anti-tumor immunity. It is currently being evaluated in a Phase I clinical trial.

old_summary: Biocytogen in collaboration with ABL Bio is developing novel bispecific antibody drug conjugates using its Renlite platform for the treatment of cancer (Press release, Biocytogen, 28 Mar 2024, https://biocytogen.com/biocytogen-enters-collaboration-with-abl-bio-to-develop-new-bispecific-antibody-drug-conjugates/#:~:text=Biocytogen%20is%20pleased%20to%20enter%20collaboration%20with%20ABL,the%20assembly%20of%20bispecific%20antibodies%20and%20bispecific%20ADCs).
new_summary: Bispecific antibody drug conjugates (bsADCs) are generated using bYlok® and GlycoConnect® technology, resulting in high yield and purity. These bsADCs have been fully analytically characterized and functionally evaluated, showing compelling tumor regression in multiple in vivo xenograft models. The combination of technologies provides first-in-class bsADCs with dual mode-of-action.

old_summary: TJ-101 is a bispecific antibody ADC, under development by Tuojie Biotech (Shanghai) for the treatment of solid tumours such as lung cancer and head and neck cancer (Wechat, Tuojie Biotech (Shanghai), 1 Jul 2024, https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/-EkXujduSgrlTTrWtNx5dw).
new_summary: TJ101 is a bispecific ADC directed against human EGFR and B7-H3, demonstrating potent anti-tumor activity and preferable toxicity profiles in multiple solid cancer types. It offers a significant advantage in overcoming potential tumor resistance.

old_summary: ADRX-0134 is a novel auristatin-based human IgG1 antibody drug conjugated at DAR8 containing AP052 tubulin inhibitor payload targeting NaPi2b, under development by Adcentrx Therapeutics for the treatment of lung and ovarian cancer (Company pipeline, 24 Jul 2024, https://www.adcentrx.com/pipeline/; Press release, Adcentrx Therapeutics, 25 Mar 2025, https://www.adcentrx.com/adcentrx-aacr-2025-presentations/).
new_summary: ADRX-0134 is a novel auristatin-based NaPi2b antibody-drug conjugate developed by Adcentrx Therapeutics. It exhibits sub-nanomolar cytotoxic activity to NaPi2b-expressing tumor cells and has shown strong efficacy and durable tumor regression in mouse models of ovarian, endometrial, and non-small cell lung cancers. ADRX-0134 achieved tumor regression in settings where other NaPi2b ADCs were insufficient and was highly tolerated in non-human primate studies.

old_summary: Affini-T Therapeutics is developing a bispecific T-cell engager using its TETHER platform for the treatment of solid tumours (Company presentation, Affini-T Therapeutics, 6 Jan 2024, Slide 8, https://affinittx.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/Affini-T-Non-Confidential-Corporate-Presentation-2024.pdf; Company pipeline, Affini-T Therapeutics, 19 Aug 2024 & 6 Oct 2025, https://affinittx.com/pipeline/).
new_summary: Affini-T Therapeutics, Inc. has developed a novel TCR-based T cell engager using their TETHER™ platform, designed to redirect the cytotoxic potential of T cells against mutant TP53-expressing cancer cells, specifically targeting the HLA-A0201 TP53-R175H mutation.

old_summary: Adimab in collaboration with Ono Pharmaceutical is developing novel bispecific antibodies, using its multi-specific antibody engineering technology for the treatment of cancer (Press release, Ono Pharmaceutical, 27 Sep 2023, https://www.ono-pharma.com/en/news/20230927.html; Company Web Page, Ono Pharmaceutical, 25 Jan 2024 & 29 Jul 2024 & 10 Sep 2025, https://www.ono-pharma.com/en/company/rd/licensing.html).
new_summary: Bispecific antibodies targeting immune checkpoints are transforming cancer immunotherapy by enabling novel mechanisms of action unattainable by monospecific antibodies.

old_summary: Sutro in collaboration with Astellas is developing an immunostimulatory antibody-drug conjugates for the treatment of cancer (Press release, Astellas Pharma, 28 Jun 2022, https://www.astellas.com/jp/news/25916; Company Pipeline, Sutro, 19 Aug 2022 & Apr 2024, https://www.sutrobio.com/pipeline/).
new_summary: By leveraging Sutro’s cell-free expression platform and its precise dual conjugation technology, the development of homogeneous dual-payload ADCs was achieved, demonstrating superior anti-tumor activities.

drug_name: PD-L1 immune-stimulating antibody conjugate, Bolt Biotherapeutics
old_summary: Bolt Biotherapeutics is developing an PD-L1 targeting immune-stimulating antibody conjugate (ISAC), for the treatment of refractory tumours, non-small cell lung cancer, breast cancer and head and neck cancer using its Boltbody ISAC platform (Company pipeline, Bolt Biotherapeutics, 24 Sep 2020 & 8 Mar 2023, https://www.boltbio.com/pipeline-new/; Company Web Page, Bolt Biotherapeutics, 24 Sep 2020, https://www.boltbio.com/immuno-oncology-platforms/; Company presentation, Bolt Biotherapeutics, 5 Mar 2021, Slide 4, https://investors.boltbio.com/static-files/a422f036-161f-48f5-8a58-b09c03b28b81; Company presentation, Bolt Biotherapeutics, 12 Sep 2023, Slide 5, https://investors.boltbio.com/static-files/d1f7527c-bd68-42a0-bfcb-ae7e818f2725).
new_summary: Bolt Biotherapeutics is developing a novel class of molecules called Immune-Stimulating Antibody Conjugates (ISACs) that target PD-L1 to engage tumor cells and antigen-presenting cells, driving robust immune responses and antitumor activity.

old_summary: Pepromene Bio is developing a BAFF-R/CD3 bispecific monoclonal antibody for the treatment of B-cell malignancies (Company pipeline, Pepromene Bio, 22 Jul 2020 & 16 Jun 2022, https://pepromenebio.com/pipeline/). It is also under development for autoimmune diseases (Company pipeline, Pepromene Bio, 20 Jun 2023 & 27 Oct 2025, https://pepromenebio.com/pipeline/).
new_summary: LY4152199 is a fully human BAFF-R×CD3 bispecific antibody that targets BAFF-R on B cells and CD3 on T cells, demonstrating potential as a first-in-class treatment for BAFF-R-positive B cell malignancies.

old_summary: Shenogen is developing bispecific antibodies, for the treatment of cancer (Company pipeline, Shenogen, 3 Jan 2020 & 13 May 2024, http://www.shenogen.com/EN/products.aspx?BaseInfoCateId=49&CateId=49).
new_summary: Bispecific antibodies targeting immune checkpoints are transforming cancer immunotherapy by enabling novel mechanisms of action unattainable by monospecific antibodies. These engineered molecules simultaneously engage two distinct epitopes to amplify anti-tumor immune responses while minimizing off-target effects.

old_summary: Magrolimab (5F9; Hu5F9-G4) is a humanized monoclonal antibody targeting CD47, under development by Ono Pharmaceutical for the treatment of pancreatic cancer and colorectal cancer (Form 10-Q, Ono Pharmaceutical, 30 Oct 2024, https://www.ono-pharma.com/sites/default/files/en/ir/library/financial_results/2025_2r_en%25_1.pdf). It was previously under development by Forty Seven for the treatment of cancer and in combination with FSI-174 for stem cell depletion as a conditioning regimen before HSCT (Press release, Forty Seven, 24 Feb 2016, http://www.fortyseveninc.com/files/8714/5626/5501/Forty-Seven-_Inc-Completes-75M-Series-A-Financing-and-Licenses-Technology-from-Stanford-University.pdf; Press release, Forty Seven, 6 Aug 2019, https://ir.fortyseveninc.com/news-releases/news-release-details/forty-seven-inc-announces-6-million-funding-leukaemia-lymphoma; ClinicalTrials.gov, 3 Nov 2016 & 23 May 2024, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT02953509). It was previously under development for stem cell depletion (Financial results document, Ono Pharmaceutical, 30 Jul 2021, Slide 15, https://www.ono-pharma.com/sites/default/files/en/ir/library/financial_results/2022_1r_en.pdf). It was also under development for triple-negative breast cancer (ClinicalTrials.gov, 12 Jul 2021, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04958785). It was also under development for melanoma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 19 May 2021, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04892446).
new_summary: Magrolimab, when given in a dose escalation manner, is well tolerated in PDX models, with evidence of efficacy in 2 of 3 models tested - AML013 and AML006 - as a single agent and in combination with chemotherapeutics. While Magro in combination with Aza or AraC resulted in statistically significant improved median survival for AML010, the difference was not clinically meaningful. Interestingly, the two models that demonstrated improvement in survival with magrolimab both harbored KMT2A rearrangements, suggesting a subset of patients that may be more responsive to the effects of CD47 blockade.

old_summary: Plamotamab (XmAb-13676) is a bispecific T-cell engager that targets CD3 & CD20, under development by Xencor using its XmAb Fc domain technology for the treatment of B-cell cancers (Press release, Xencor, 31 Jul 2014; Press release, Xencor, 26 Jun 2015; Company pipeline, Xencor, 6 Nov 2018). It is also under development for diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, non-Hodgkin lymphoma and follicular lymphoma (FL) (Press release, Xencor, 5 Nov 2020, https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20201105006063/en/Xencor-Reports-Third-Quarter-2020-Financial-Results; Company presentation, Xencor, 5 May 2021, Slide 6, https://investors.xencor.com/static-files/243d3a74-a45c-4796-a9c8-a2d0b44b9181). It is also under development for multi-drug resistant rheumatoid arthritis (Company pipeline, Xencor, 20 Sep 2024, https://xencor.com/pipeline/plamotamab/).
new_summary: Plamotamab is a CD20 x CD3 bispecific antibody evaluated in a multi-center, multi-stage Phase 1 study. It was administered intravenously and subcutaneously, showing clinical activity and tolerability in heavily pretreated patients with relapsed/refractory non-Hodgkin's lymphoma. The subcutaneous dosing demonstrated favorable pharmacokinetics with a lower incidence and severity of cytokine release syndrome compared to intravenous administration.

old_summary: TJ-101 is a bispecific antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) targeting EGFR and B7-H3 with a cleavable linker and a novel topoisomerase I inhibitor, under development by Phrontline Biopharma for the treatment of solid tumours (China FDA, CTR20253279, 15 Aug 2025, http://www.chinadrugtrials.org.cn/clinicaltrials.searchlistdetail.dhtml; Company Pipeline, Phrontline Biopharma, 19 Aug 2025, https://phrontlinebio.com/Our_Pipeline.html; ClinicalTrials.gov, 24 Sep 2025, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT07181473).
new_summary: TJ101 is a bispecific ADC targeting EGFR and B7-H3, demonstrating potent anti-tumor activity and preferable toxicity profiles in multiple solid cancer types. It offers a significant advantage in overcoming potential tumor resistance.

old_summary: FP-008 is first in class PD-1 antibody and IL-10M cytokine (antibody-cytokine) fusion protein under development by Fapon Biopharma for the treatment of solid tumours (Press release, Fapon Biopharma, 4 Jun 2024, https://www.faponbiopharma.com/newsInfo-86.html; Company pipeline, Fapon Biopharma, 30 Jun 2024, https://www.faponbiopharma.com/yfgx.html; Press release, Fapon Biopharma, 28 Feb 2025, https://www.prnewswire.co.uk/news-releases/fapon-biopharma-announces-fda-approval-of-ind-for-fp008-a-first-in-class-immunotherapy-for-solid-tumors-302388383.html).
new_summary: FP008 is a novel anti-PD-1 × IL-10M fusion protein developed for cancer therapy. It demonstrated strong anti-tumor efficacy, decreased CD8+ T cell apoptosis, and promoted IFN-γ secretion. FP008 showed improved safety profiles and induced immunological memory in preclinical models.

old_summary: ABBV-969 is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting PSMA/STEAP1 with a proprietary, cytotoxic topoisomerase 1 inhibitor (Top1i) payload, under development by AbbVie for the treatment of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (Company presentation, AbbVie, 30 Nov 2023, Slide 10 & 11, https://investors.abbvie.com/static-files/a82f2f5d-2c9c-4288-aad0-ac2061cada8f; ClinicalTrials.gov, 19 Mar 2024 and 24 Oct 2024, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06318273; Company pipeline, AbbVie, 21 Feb 2025, https://www.abbvie.com/science/pipeline.html; Press release, AbbVie, 26 Mar 2025, https://news.abbvie.com/2025-03-26-AbbVie-Showcases-Early-Pipeline-and-Scientific-Advances-in-Oncology-at-AACR-Annual-Meeting-2025).
new_summary: First-in-human study of ABBV-969, a dual variable antibody-drug conjugate (ADC), in patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC).

old_summary: ABBV-514 is a CCR-8 targeting immuno-oncology therapy, under development by AbbVie for non-small cell lung cancer, head and neck squamous cell carcinoma and other solid tumours (ClinicalTrials.gov, 13 Aug 2021, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05005403; JP Morgan 40th Ann Healthcare Conf (Virtual), 2022, Slide 16, https://investors.abbvie.com/static-files/6eaf137c-4bee-4a5d-8047-33b9f299382d; Company Web Page, AbbVie, 25 Apr 2022, https://www.abbvie.com/our-science/pipeline/abbv-514.html; JP Morgan 41st Ann Healthcare Conf (California), 2023, Slide 9, https://investors.abbvie.com/static-files/4d7aae8a-65ca-45f9-bc0f-a169b5a7dc9d). It is under development as a single agent and in combination with a PD-1 inhibitor (Company pipeline, AbbVie, Apr 2022 & 10 Apr 2023 & 21 Feb 2025, https://www.abbvie.com/science/pipeline.html).
new_summary: ABBV-514 is a human CCR8 specific afucosylated hIgG1 antibody that targets and eliminates key immunosuppressive tumor regulatory T cells, enhancing anti-tumor immunity.

old_summary: Nadunolimab (CAN-04) is a fully humanized anti-interleukin-1 receptor accessory protein (IL1RAP) MAb, under development by Cantargia by using the bioWa proprietary POTELLIGENT technology for the treatment of cancer including colorectal cancer, non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), pancreatic cancer and triple-negative breast cancer. It works by stimulating the natural killer (NK) cells of the immune system to destroy the tumour cells by a process called antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity (ADCC) (Press release, Cantargia, 2 Feb 2012; Press release, Cantargia, 30 Jan 2012; Company Web Page, Cantargia, 5 Feb 2013, https://cantargia.com/en/research-development/our-projects/can04?scroll=500; Press release, Cantargia, 19 Jun 2018, http://cantargia.com/en/press-releases/cantargias-immuno-oncology-antibody-can04-has-obtained-the-inn-nidanilimab-by-who; Press release, Cantargia, 23 Aug 2019, https://cantargia.com/en/press-releases/cantargia-and-biowa-extend-ongoing-collaboration-around-the-potelligent-technology). It is also under development for head and neck squamous cell cancer and bladder cancer. It was previously under development for the treatment of chronic myelogenous leukaemia and acute myelogenous leukaemia. (Press release, Cantargia, 15 Nov 2019, https://cantargia.com/en/press-releases/cantargia-advance-antibody-can04-development-after-positive-pre-ind-meeting; Company pipeline, Cantargia, 19 Jun 2020, https://cantargia.com/en/research-development/our-projects; Press release, Cantargia, 27 May 2020, https://cantargia.com/en/press-releases/cantargia-publishes-interim-report-for-first-quarter-2020). It is also under development for the treatment of myelodysplastic syndrome (Press release, Cantargia, 12 Mar 2025, https://cantargia.com/en/press-releases/first-patient-enrolled-in-cantargias-leukemia-study-with-nadunolimab).
new_summary: Phase I/II trial shows promising efficacy of nadunolimab in combination with platinum doublet as second-line therapy in patients with NSCLC.

old_summary: Bexmarilimab (FP-1305) is a humanized antibody against Clever-1 (stabilin-1), under development by Faron Pharmaceuticals using tumour immunity enabling technology (TIET), for the treatment of breast, colorectal, melanoma, gastrointestinal, ovarian, pancreatic and hepatocellular carcinoma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 7 Nov 2018 & 25 Mar 2022, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT03733990). It is also under development for haematological malignancies (Company pipeline, Faron, 3 Feb 2021 & 27 Jun 2022, https://www.faron.com/pipeline). It was previously under development for inflammation lung cancer, biliary cancer and renal cell cancer (Press release, Faron, 5 Sep 2016; Press release, Faron pharmaceuticals, 7 May 2019; ClinicalTrials.gov, 3 Nov 2021, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05104905). It is also under development for acute myelogenous leukaemia and myelodysplastic syndrome (Press release, Faron Pharmaceuticals, 16 May 2022, https://www.faron.com/news-events/news-and-press-releases?rnsid=1583587&cid=2223).
new_summary: Bexmarilimab, a macrophage checkpoint inhibitor, is being studied in combination with azacitidine for the treatment of refractory or relapsed myelodysplastic syndrome (r/r MDS). It is well tolerated and shows promising clinical efficacy, with an objective response rate of 79% in the study population.

old_summary: BioNTech is developing a combination of BNT-327, a PD-L1/VEGF-A targeting bispecific antibody with BNT-325/DB-1305, a Trop-2 targeting ADC with a novel topoisomerase I inhibitor P1021 for the treatment of solid tumours (1st qtr rep, BioNTech, 5 May 2025, Page 31, https://investors.biontech.de/system/files-encrypted/nasdaq_kms/assets/2025/05/05/9-46-14/Q1%202025.pdf; Company pipeline, BioNTech, 12 Aug 2025, https://www.biontech.com/content/biontech-corporate/int/en/home/pipeline-and-products/pipeline.html).
new_summary: BNT327+BNT325 is a combination therapy involving BNT327, an investigational anti-PD-L1 x VEGF-A bispecific antibody, and BNT325, an investigational anti-TROP2 ADC. Preclinical models have shown that this combination demonstrates superior tumor growth inhibition compared to each treatment alone. Early clinical data from a phase 1/2a study in patients with advanced solid tumors indicate successful dose escalation and no dose-limiting toxicities, with stomatitis being the most notable adverse event.

old_summary: HuM195-Ac-225 is an alpha-emitter-coupled monoclonal antibody (MAb), under development by Actinium Pharmaceuticals for the treatment of acute myeloid leukaemia (AML). It was also under development for multiple myeloma and myelodysplastic syndrome (Company Web Page, Actinium, 7 Jun 2007). It consists of the CD33 targeted antibody, Lintuzumab linked to the actinium-225 payload (Press release, Actinium, 24 Jun 2014; Company pipeline, Actinium, 19 Sep 2017, http://www.actiniumpharma.com/product-pipeline). It is also under development for solid tumours (Press release, Actinium Pharmaceuticals, 19 Mar 2025, https://ir.actiniumpharma.com/press-releases/detail/494/actinium-pharmaceuticals-to-host-investor-kol-call-with-dr).
new_summary: Lintuzumab-Ac225 is a promising investigational antibody radio-conjugate targeting CD33, showing potent anti-leukemic activity in AML cell lines regardless of mutation status. It enhances treatment response when combined with standard therapies, supporting its potential as a backbone therapy for relapsed/refractory AML.

old_summary: AZD-5335 is an FRalpha-TOP1 antibody-drug conjugate, under development by AstraZeneca for the treatment of solid tumours and ovarian cancer (ClinicalTrials.gov, 4 Apr 2023, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05797168; Company presentation, AstraZeneca, 27 Apr 2023, Slide 11, https://www.astrazeneca.com//content/dam/az/Investor_Relations/events/IR-AACR-2023-Highlights-Brochure.pdf). It is also under development for lung adenocarcinoma (Company pipeline, AstraZeneca, 28 Jul 2023 & 25 Apr 2024, https://www.astrazeneca.com/our-therapy-areas/pipeline.html). It is also under development for non-small cell lung cancer (Company presentation, AstraZeneca, 21 May 2024, Slide 32, https://www.astrazeneca.com/content/dam/az/Investor_Relations/events/ID/Oncology-presentation.pdf).
new_summary: AZD5335, a folate receptor alpha-targeted antibody-drug conjugate, enhances ovarian cancer treatment with bevacizumab, paclitaxel, and/or carboplatin, showing significant and durable anti-tumor responses in pre-clinical models.

old_summary: Belantamab (GSK-2857914; Bela) is an anti-BCMA antibody, under development by GlaxoSmithKline for the treatment of multiple myeloma (Molecular Cancer Therapeutics, 1 Oct 2021, https://aacrjournals.org/mct/article/20/10/1941/665728/Belantamab-Mafodotin-GSK2857916-Drives-Immunogenic; ClinicalTrials.gov, 6 Feb 2023, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05714839; Company pipeline, GlaxoSmithKline, 15 Apr 2024, https://www.gsk.com/en-gb/innovation/pipeline/). It is also under development for the treatment of autoimmune diseases (ClinicalTrials.gov, 14 May 2024 & 3 Jun 2025, https://www.clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06413511).
new_summary: Belantamab mafodotin is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting BCMA that was withdrawn from the market but recent phase III trials have shown superior outcomes with belantamab-based regimens, indicating a potential comeback.

old_summary: SAR-445877 (KD-050) is a fusion protein comprised of anti-PD-1 antibody-IL-15 mutein fusion protein, under development by Kadmon Holdings (acquired by Sanofi) for the treatment of solid tumours (ClinicalTrials.gov, 18 Oct 2022, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05584670; Pres release, Sanofi, 3 Feb 2023, https://www.sanofi.com/dam/jcr:0e9df19c-7a67-47de-83ad-19e63173b0cb/2023_02_03_Q4_FY_2022_SANOFI_Press_Release_EN.pdf; Company pipeline, Sanofi, 3 Feb 2023, https://www.sanofi.com/en/science-and-innovation/research-and-development/rd-pipeline; American Association of Cancer Research, ABS 2972, 4 Apr 2023, https://aacrjournals.org/cancerres/article/83/7_Supplement/2972/721765). It is also under development for the treatment of non-small cell lung cancer (ClinicalTrials.gov, 21 Aug 2025, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT07133425).
new_summary: SAR-445877, formerly KD050, is an anti-PD-1 antibody-IL-15 mutein fusion protein with robust anti-tumor efficacy as monotherapy. It shows superior efficacy to single agent PD-1 or untargeted IL-15 in mouse tumor models.

old_summary: Cevostamab (RG-6160) is an investigational bispecific T-cell engager (BiTE) directed against CD3 and FCRH5, under development by Genentech (Roche) for the treatment of multiple myeloma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 11 Sep 2017, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT03275103; NIH Drug Dictionary, 11 Sep 2018, https://www.cancer.gov/publications/dictionaries/cancer-drug/def/anti-fcrh5-cd3-bite-antibody-bfcr4350a; Company pipeline, Genentech, 19 Feb 2019, https://www.gene.com/medical-professionals/pipeline; Press release, Roche, 8 Dec 2020, https://www.roche.com/media/releases/med-cor-2020-12-08.htm; Company presentation, Roche, 6 Jun 2022, Slide 64, https://assets.cwp.roche.com/f/126832/x/2adb205911/ir-asco-2022-event.pdf).
new_summary: Cevostamab is a FcRH5xCD3 bispecific antibody that demonstrates clinically meaningful activity and manageable safety in patients with heavily pretreated relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (RRMM). In ongoing studies, cevostamab has shown efficacy at the 160mg target-dose level, with an overall response rate of 43.1% in one study and 60% in another cohort of patients who had received prior BCMA-targeted therapies.

old_summary: Farletuzumab ecteribulin (MORAb-202; BMS-986445; FZEC) is an antibody-drug conjugate of farletuzumab, an anti-folate receptor alpha antibody, with eribulin, under development by Eisai using Morphotek's organic synthesis technology for the treatment of cancer (1st Qtr Rep, Eisai, 10 May 2017, Slide 20, http://www.eisai.com/pdf/eir/emat/e4523_170510.pdf; Company pipeline, Eisai, 1 Nov 2018, 5 Aug 2022 & 3 Feb 2023, Page 4, https://www.eisai.com/company/business/research/pdf/epipeline.pdf; 2nd Qtr res, Eisai, 30 Oct 2019, Slide 25; Company presentation, Eisai, 7 Nov 2023, Slide 33, https://www.eisai.com/ir/library/presentations/pdf/e4523_231107.pdf). It is also under development for platinum-resistant high-grade serous ovarian, primary peritoneal and fallopian tube cancers (ClinicalTrials.gov, 14 Nov 2022 & 5 Apr 2023, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05613088). It is also under development for non-small cell lung cancer (ClinicalTrials.gov, 26 Oct 2022, 24 Nov 2022 & 7 Apr 2023, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05577715).
new_summary: Farletuzumab ecteribulin (FZEC) is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting folate receptor alpha (FRα) and has demonstrated dose-dependent antitumor effects in endometrial cancer patient-derived xenograft models. The study showed significant antitumor effects even in FRα-low or negative models, with intratumoral eribulin accumulation correlating strongly with efficacy.

old_summary: Zilovertamab vedotin (MK-2140) is a receptor tyrosine kinase-like orphan receptor 1 (ROR1) directed antibody-drug conjugate (ADC), with an MMAE payload, under development by Merck & Co. (VelosBio before the acquisition) for the treatment of haematological malignancies such as diffuse large B-cell lymphoma and solid tumours. It was previously under development for mantle cell lymphoma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 6 Feb 2019, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT03833180; Press release, VelosBio, 8 Jul 2020; Press release, VelosBio, 31 Aug 2020; Press release, Merck, 5 Oct 2020, https://www.merck.com/news/merck-to-acquire-velosbio/; Company presentation, Merck & Co., 20 Sep 2021, Slide 30, https://s21.q4cdn.com/488056881/files/doc_presentations/2021/ESMO-Investor-Event-Slides.pdf). It is also under development for the treatment of breast, chronic lymphocytic leukaemia, gastric, ovarian, pancreatic, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, non-small cell lung cancer and urothelial carcinoma (Company pipeline, Merck & Co., 17 Feb 2023, Slide 3, https://www.merck.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/5/2023/03/Public-Pipeline_1Q23.pdf; ClinicalTrials.gov, 29 Aug 2022, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05458297; ClinicalTrials.gov, 3 Oct 2022, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05562830; JP Morgan 42nd Ann Healthcare Conf (California), 2024, Slide 10, https://s21.q4cdn.com/488056881/files/doc_events/2024/Jan/09/mrk-jp-morgan-healthcare-conference-2024-for-website.pdf). It is also under development for acute lymphoblastic leukaemia, Ewing's sarcoma and neuroblastoma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 1 May 2024, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06395103).
new_summary: Zilovertamabvedotin is a ROR1-targeting antibody-drug conjugate that has demonstrated clinically meaningful antitumor activity and manageable safety in patients with relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (R/R DLBCL) and mantle cell lymphoma (R/R MCL).

old_summary: Elritercept (KER-050; TAK-226), an engineered ligand trap comprised of a modified ligand-binding domain of the TGF-ß receptor known as activin receptor type IIA (ActRIIA) that is fused to the portion of the human antibody known as the Fc domain, is under development by Keros for the treatment of anaemia and thrombocytopenia in patients with myelodysplastic syndromes and myelofibrosis. It was previously under development for the treatment of multiple muscle disorders including Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) and facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy (FSHD) (Company pipeline, Keros, 8 May 2020, https://www.kerostx.com/our-pipeline; Company Web Page, Keros, 8 May 2020, https://www.kerostx.com/our-leads; Press release, Keros Therapeutics, 7 Jan 2019; ANZCTR Registry, ACTRN12619000318189, 5 Jun 2019, https://www.anzctr.org.au/Trial/Registration/TrialReview.aspx?id=376970).
new_summary: Elritercept is an investigational drug being evaluated for its potential to address ineffective hematopoiesis by inhibiting activin A and other TGF-β superfamily ligands, showing promise in increasing erythropoiesis and thrombopoiesis in preclinical studies. It is currently being studied in a Phase 3 trial for the treatment of anemia in adults with lower-risk MDS.

old_summary: Patritumab deruxtecan (MK-1022, U3-1402; HER3-DXd) is a HER3-targeting antibody conjugated to a topoisomerase I inhibitor exatecan derivative, under development by Daiichi Sankyo for the treatment of breast and non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) (Japan Clinical Trial Registry, 163401, 17 Oct 2016, http://www.clinicaltrials.jp/user/showCteDetailE.jsp?japicId=JapicCTI-163401;Company pipeline, Daiichi Sankyo, 1 Oct 2016, Page 1,4 & 5, http://www.daiichisankyo.com/rd/pipeline/pdf/20161031_Pipeline_EN.pdf; Company presentation, Daiichi, 6 Jun 2017, http://www.daiichisankyo.com/media_investors/investor_relations/ir_calendar/files/005349/Coference%20Call%20Material.pdf). U3-1402 consists of an antibody component (patritumab) covalently conjugated to a drug-linker (MAAA-1162a) containing a drug component (MAAA-1181a) (ClinicalTrials.gov, 12 Jul 2018, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT03260491). It was also under development for solid tumours (2nd Qtr Res, Daiichi Sankyo, 31 Oct 2018, Slide 35, https://www.daiichisankyo.com/media_investors/investor_relations/ir_calendar/files/005402/DS%20FY2018Q2_Presentation%20Material.pdf). It was previously under development for colorectal cancer (ClinicalTrials.gov, 21 Jul 2020 & 13 Jan 2023, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04479436; Company pipeline, Merck & Co., 15 Nov 2023, https://www.merck.com/research/product-pipeline/). It is also under development for gastrointestinal cancers (ClinicalTrials.gov, 22 Oct 2024, https://www.clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06596694).
new_summary: Patritumab deruxtecan (U3-1402) is the only HER3-targeting therapy that has demonstrated clinical efficacy in non-small cell lung cancer, but it is ineffective in more challenging tumor types, including those with low HER3 expression.

old_summary: Linvoseltamab (REGN-5458) is an anti-BCMA and anti-CD3 bispecific antibody, developed by Regeneron for the treatment of relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 3 Dec 2018, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT03761108; Form 10-K, Regeneron, 31 Dec 2018, Page 6 & 9, https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/872589/000153217619000009/regn-123118x10k.htm; Press release, Regeneron, 12 Dec 2022, https://investor.regeneron.com//news-releases/news-release-details/linvoseltamab-bcmaxcd3-initial-pivotal-phase-2-data-show; Press release, Regeneron, 28 Apr 2025, https://investor.regeneron.com/news-releases/news-release-details/lynozyfictm-linvoseltamab-approved-european-union-treatment). Development as a SC formulation is planned (Company presentation, Regeneron, 5 Feb 2021, Slide 27, https://investor.regeneron.com/index.php/static-files/a581c03d-e314-4ce2-8ca9-bc182beaebc9). It is also under development for chronic kidney disease (CKD) (ClinicalTrials.gov, 25 Oct 2021, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05092347). It is also under development for relapsed or refractory systemic light chain amyloidosis (ClinicalTrials.gov, 5 Mar 2024, https://classic.clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT06292780). It is also under development for food allergy (ClinicalTrials.gov, 17 Apr 2024 & 30 Apr 2024, https://www.clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06369467).
new_summary: Linvoseltamab is a fully human BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibody that demonstrated high and durable responses in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (RRMM). It retains its binding and functional activity even in the presence of an identified BCMA mutation (R27P) that negatively impacts other BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibodies, suggesting it may be less susceptible to resistance mechanisms.

old_summary: Belantamab mafodotin (GSK-2857916) is a humanized immunoglobulin G1 (IgG1) anti-BCMA antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) with an MMAF payload, developed by Pfizer (Seagen before acquisition) in collaboration with GlaxoSmithKline for the treatment of multiple myeloma (Company pipeline, Seattle Genetics, 25 Jan 2012, 15 Oct 2018 & 9 Oct 2019, http://www.seattlegenetics.com/science-technology/adc-technology/adc-collaborations; JP Morgan 30th Ann Healthcare Conf (San Francisco), 2012, Slide 21 of 24; Press release, Seattle Genetics, 30 Oct 2014; JP Morgan 33nd Annual Healthcare Conf (San Francisco), 2015, Slide 18; 1st qtr press release, GSK, 1 May 2019, Page 4, https://www.gsk.com/media/5550/q1-2019-results-announcement.pdf). It is a novel Fc-engineered anti-B cell maturation antigen-monomethyl auristatin F antibody-drug conjugate via uncleavable linker (55th Meet Am Soc Hematol (New Orleans), 2013, Abs 877; ClinicalTrials.gov, 19 Feb 2014, http://clinicaltrials.gov/show/NCT02064387). It is also under development for autoimmune disease (ClinicalTrials.gov, 11 Mar 2025, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06413511).
new_summary: Belantamab mafodotin, an antibody-drug conjugate targeting the B-cell maturation antigen, has shown significant efficacy in patients with relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma and AL amyloidosis. Ocular adverse events were the most common Grade ≥3 safety finding observed.

old_summary: Motixafortide (BKT-140; T-140) is a selective CXCR4 antagonist peptide, under development by Biokine Therapeutics, for the treatment of cancer and cancer-related side-effects such as myeloma and other haematological diseases. It blocks the interaction between CXCL12 and CXCR4 by binding to CXCR4 with a long receptor occupancy (>48 hours) (Company Web Page, Biokine Therapeutics, 20 Mar 2009; 51st Meet Am Soc Hematol (New Orleans), 2009, Abs 3857; Company pipeline, Biokine Therapeutics, 7 Jul 2021, https://biokine.com/pipeline/pre-clinical/). It is also under development for the treatment of chronic myeloid leukaemia (Press release, BioLineRx, 13 Nov 2013, http://www.biolinerx.com/default.asp?pageid=16&itemid=238). It is also under development for the treatment of aplastic anaemia, myelodysplastic syndrome and pancreatic cancer (Press release, BioLineRx, 12 Dec 2014, http://www.biolinerx.com/default.asp?pageid=16&itemid=311; Press release, BioLineRx, 12 Jan 2016, http://www.biolinerx.com/default.asp?pageid=16&itemid=385). It was previously being investigated for myeloma, radio/chemotherapy-induced anaemia/injury, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma and acute lymphocytic leukaemia (Company Web Page, Biokine Therapeutics, 7 Feb 2019, http://biokine.com/). It is also under development for acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) secondary to COVID-19 and other respiratory viral infections and cytokine storm that is associated with poor COVID-19 infection outcomes (Press release, BioLineRx, 18 Nov 2020, https://ir.biolinerx.com/news-releases/news-release-details/biolinerx-announces-initiation-phase-1b-clinical-trial-patients).
new_summary: Motixafortide is a high affinity CXCR4 antagonist used for mobilizing CD34+ hematopoietic stem cells in multiple myeloma patients undergoing autologous stem cell transplantation. The phase 3 GENESIS trial showed that 88.8% of patients mobilized the required number of stem cells in one procedure with motixafortide and G-CSF, leading to its FDA approval in September 2023. The implementation of a modified premedication protocol, MF-DEX, reduced hypersensitivity reactions associated with motixafortide.

old_summary: Luspatercept (ACE-536) is a soluble receptor fusion protein consisting of a modified activin receptor Type IIB extracellular domain linked to a human Fc domain, developed by Acceleron Pharma (a subsidiary of Merck & Co.), for the treatment of anaemia, including chemotherapy-induced anaemia, myelodysplastic syndrome, thalassaemia and myelofibrosis. It was previously under development for renal anaemia and sickle cell anaemia. It is a non-erythropoietin, non-HIF-1alpha anti-anaemic (Press release, Acceleron, 6 Jan 2009; 53rd Meet Am Soc Hematol (San Diego), 2011, Abs 610, http://ash.confex.com/ash/2011/webprogram/Paper44286.html: Company pipeline, BMS, 3 Jun 2014 & 5 May 2023, https://www.bmsscience.com/pipeline-trials/). It increases RBC levels by inhibiting members in the TGF-ß superfamily (Company Web Page, Acceleron, 2 Feb 2013; Press release, Merck & Co., 22 Nov 2021, https://www.merck.com/news/merck-completes-acquisition-of-acceleron-pharma-inc/).
new_summary: Luspatercept is a first-in-class erythroid maturation agent approved for patients with lower risk MDS with ring sideroblasts and transfusion dependent anemia. It has shown efficacy in achieving red blood cell transfusion independence and increasing hemoglobin levels in patients. The LusPlus trial is evaluating the efficacy and safety of initiating luspatercept at a maximum dose of 1.75 mg/kg, with preliminary results indicating it is well tolerated and may improve response rates.

old_summary: Sacituzumab govitecan (GS-0132) is an immunoconjugate of humanized MAb hRS7 and SN-38 the active metabolite of irinotecan, developed by Immunomedics using its DOCK-AND-LOCK (DNL) protein conjugation technology, for the treatment of cancer. The conjugate is designed to selectively deliver SN-38 to tumours, increasing the amount of drug reaching the tumours while minimizing the damage to other tissues and organs (Press release, Immunomedics, 25 Oct 2007; Press release, Immunomedics, 20 May 2014, http://www.immunomedics.com/pdfs/news/2014/pr05202014.pdf; Press release, Immunomedics, 20 Oct 2014). Two improved bifunctional immunoconjugates, consisting of hRS7 conjugated to CL2-SN-38 and its ethyl carbonate CL2-SN-38(Et) are also under investigation (100th AACR (Denver), 2009, Abs 3233). The antibody RS7 targets the TROP-2 antigen which is conjugated to SN-38 (ClinicalTrials.gov, 3 Jul 2012, http://www.clinicaltrials.gov/show/NCT01631552). It is also under development for the treatment of breast, gastrointestinal, small cell lung cancer, ovarian, pancreatic, and prostate cancer (Press release, Immunomedics, 8 Nov 2013, http://www.immunomedics.com/pdfs/news/2013/pr11082013.pdf; WHO INN LIst, 21 Jul 2017). It is also under development for the treatment of glioblastoma (Press release, Immunomedics, 12 Oct 2020).
new_summary: Sacituzumab govitecan, an antibody-drug conjugate targeting Trop-2, was studied in combination with L-NMMA for treating inflammatory breast cancer, showing potential synergistic effects in reducing tumor growth.

old_summary: Tafasitamab (XmAb-5574; ICP-B04) is an anti-CD19 humanized MAb containing an XmAb Fc domain, developed by Incyte, for the treatment of cancer and autoimmune diseases. It was previously under development by Novartis (MorphoSys before acquisition) (in-licensed from Xencor) (Press release, Xencor, 15 Apr 2008; Company Web Page, Xencor, 11 Feb 2010; Form 10-Q, Xencor, 1 Aug 2014, Page 18, http://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1326732/000110465914056069/a14-14228_110q.htm; Company pipeline, Xencor, 5 Nov 2014, http://www.xencor.com/pipeline/ & MorphoSys, 31 Mar 2015, http://www.morphosys.com/pipeline; Scrip Intelligence, 17 May 2019; Interim results, InnoCare Pharma, Aug 2021, Slide 10, https://www.innocarepharma.com/media/1602/innocare-interim-ndr_en_2021829_final-de.pdf; Press release, Incyte, 5 Feb 2024, https://investor.incyte.com/news-releases/news-release-details/incyte-gains-exclusive-global-development-and-commercialization). It is also under development for autoimmune hemolytic anaemia and immune thrombocytopenic purpura (ClinicalTrials.gov,5 Aug 2025, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT07104565).
new_summary: Tafasitamab, a CD19-directed monoclonal antibody, is used in combination with lenalidomide as a chemotherapy-free treatment for adults with R/R DLBCL ineligible for autologous stem cell transplant. Studies show that CD19 expression is retained after tafasitamab treatment, suggesting potential for subsequent CD19-targeting therapies.

old_summary: Enfortumab vedotin (AGS-22M6E) is an antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) comprising the highly potent microtubule-disrupting agent monomethyl auristatin E (MMAE) conjugated to an anti-nectin-4 fully-human monoclonal antibody (MAb) for AGS-22, a cell surface antigen, developed by Agensys (Astellas) for the treatment of bladder cancer. It was previously under development for the treatment of multiple solid tumours including breast cancer, lung cancer, pancreatic cancer, head and neck cancer, and gastric and esophageal adenocarcinoma (Company Web Page, Astellas, 16 May 2011, http://www.astellas.us/therapeutic/rnd/agensys.html; Company pipeline, Astellas, 25 Apr 2019 & 29 Oct 2020 & 25 Apr 2024, Page 2, https://www.astellas.com/en/system/files/94eee64571/4q2023_pip_en.pdf; Company presentation, Astellas, 30 Jul 2025, https://www.astellas.com/en/system/files/3a6a1a2221/1q2025_pre_en.pdf). It is also under development for colorectal cancer and hepatocellular carcinoma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 25 Mar 2025, https://www.clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06553885). It is also under development for the treatment of melanoma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 2 Apr 2025, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06906822).
new_summary: Efficacy of enfortumab vedotin (EV) in patients with advanced urothelial carcinoma who have baseline neuropathy and/or diabetes mellitus was analyzed in the UNITE study.

old_summary: Mogamulizumab (KW-0761) is a fucosylated humanized monoclonal antibody (MAb), developed by Biowa (Kyowa Hakko Kirin; Kyowa Hakko before the merger)) using its Potelligent technology, for the treatment of allergy and T-cell lymphoma. It targets CCR4 chemokine receptors selectively expressed in T-helper Type 2 (Th2) cells (Company Rep, Kyowa, 28 Aug 2006). A companion diagnostic was co-developed with mogamulizumab to determine the CCR4 expression in ATL cells of patients. Development for human T-lymphotropic virus infection was discontinued (Company pipeline, Kyowa Kirin, 30 Jun 2021, https://www.kyowakirin.com/what_we_do/index.html#anc-pipeline).
new_summary: Mogamulizumab is a defucosylated humanized anti-CCR4 antibody approved in the United States for the treatment of patients with relapsed or refractory MF or SS. A retrospective cohort study analyzed data from mogamulizumab-treated patients, showing that the duration of mogamulizumab treatment was similar in the real world compared to the results observed in the MAVORIC clinical trial. The study also found that the duration of mogamulizumab treatment was longer in patients receiving combination treatment than those on monotherapy.

old_summary: Brentuximab vedotin (SGN-35) is an antibody-linked drug conjugate (ADC), developed by Seagen (formerly Seattle Genetics) for the treatment of haematologic malignancies, including Hodgkin's lymphoma (HD) and some types of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma (NHL) expressing CD30, such as anaplastic large cell lymphoma (ALCL). It consists of an anti-CD30 MAb (SGN-30) attached to monomethyl auristatin E (MMAE) via a protease-sensitive linker (Company Web Page, Seattle, 28 Oct 2002; Press release, Seattle, 24 Jul 2009). Auristatin E is a synthetic analogue of the natural product dolastatin-10, and prevents cell division by the inhibition of tubulin polymerization. A previous lead in the series, SGN-25, was under development for carcinoma (Company Web Page, Seattle, 14 Jun 2002). Brentuximab vedotin also previously showed potential in autoimmune disorders such as multiple sclerosis and systemic lupus erythematosus (Company Web Page; 28 Oct 2002). A companion diagnostic has been co-developed with brentuximab vedotin to evaluate CD30 expression levels in tissue specimens (Press release, Takeda, 12 Apr 2012; Company pipeline, Seagen, 9 Oct 2019 & 5 May 2023, https://www.seagen.com/science/pipeline). It was previously under development for HIV (JP Morgan 40th Ann Healthcare Conf (Virtual), 2022, Slide 8 & 26, https://s21.q4cdn.com/327105422/files/doc_presentations/2022/Seagen-2022-JPM-Presentation-Final.pdf).
new_summary: Brentuximab vedotin (BV) was evaluated in combination with lenalidomide for efficacy and safety in a phase II trial involving patients with relapsed/refractory PTCL/CTCL. The study found that the combination demonstrated efficacy comparable to each agent used as monotherapy, with an overall response rate of 27.8% for CTCL and 50% for PTCL.

old_summary: Inotuzumab ozogamicin is an anti-CD22 antibody-calicheamicin conjugate, developed by Wyeth (Pfizer) (formerly American Home Products) for the treatment of acute lymphocytic leukaemia (Company Web Page, Celltech, 29 Apr 2002; Company pipeline, Pfizer, 28 Apr 2015, Page 8; Company Web Page, Pfizer, Mar 2021, https://www.pfizer.com/products-list?letter=B). Development for indolent and aggressive non-Hodgkin's lymphoma was discontinued. It was previously under development by UCB (formerly Celltech) (Company pipeline, Pfizer, 9 Aug 2012, Page 12).
new_summary: Inotuzumab ozogamicin (InO) is an FDA-approved antibody-drug conjugate used for the treatment of adults and children with relapsed/refractory BCP-ALL. A significant finding from the pediatric trial ITCC-059 is that sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (SOS) occurred in 13% of patients treated with InO, and in 20% of patients after a subsequent transplant. The time between the last InO dose and hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) is a significant risk factor for developing SOS.

old_summary: Blinatumomab (MT-103) is a bispecific T-cell engager (BiTE) molecule, CD19 and CD3, developed by Micromet (Amgen) for the treatment of B-cell tumours such as leukaemia and lymphoma and refractory systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with nephritis (Study abstract, Dec 2012, http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0163725812001830; Scrip Daily Online, 26 Jan 2004, S00830520; USAN Web Page, 7 May 2008; Company pipeline, Astellas, May 2015, http://www.astellas.com/en/ir/library/pdf/4q2015_rd_en.pdf; 43rd JP Morgan Healthcare Conference, Jan 13-16, 2025, Slide 22, https://investors.amgen.com/static-files/f27ce84a-d187-493b-8a0d-2785fa2d7ce2). It is derived from murine MAbs. BiTE molecules bind to both the target cell and T-cells, and can recruit and activate T-cells without the need for any co-stimulatory signal. Other BiTE molecules include MT-110 and MT-107 (both qv) (Scrip Daily Online, 26 Jan 2004, S00830520). As it has a very short t1/2, continuous infusion is required (48th Meet Am Soc Hematol (Orlando), 2006, Abs 693). It is also under development for rheumatoid arthritis (ClinicalTrials.gov, 10 Jan 2025 & 12 Mar 2025, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06570798).
new_summary: Blinatumomab, a bispecific T-cell engager, has shown efficacy in treating B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B-ALL) by targeting CD19 on B-cells and CD3 on T-cells. It has demonstrated promising results in both pediatric and adult patients, improving survival rates and achieving deep remission in various clinical settings.

old_summary:
Cetuximab (C225) is a chimaeric MAb specific for epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR), developed by ImClone Systems (Lilly) for cancers overexpressing EGFR. It inhibited the receptor-associated tyrosine kinase, thus blocking the intracellular signalling pathway that results in cell proliferation (Company Web Page, Eli Lilly, 9 Apr 2018; Direct communication, ImClone, 22 Mar 2001). ImClone is also separately developing a non-chimaeric version of cetuximab (necitumumab).
 A companion diagnostic had been developed to identify epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) expression in normal and neoplastic tissues in colorectal cancer patients (Company web page, Dako, 6 Apr 2012). Development for gastric cancer and adjuvant colon cancer was discontinued (2nd Qtr Fin Res, Merck Serono, 14 Aug 2012, Page 31; Company Half Year Financial Report, Merck Serono, 2012, Page 9).
new_summary: Cetuximab is an EGFR-targeting monoclonal antibody used in the treatment of colorectal cancer. It has been shown to increase LGR5 protein levels and enhance the efficacy of LGR5 antibody-drug conjugates in colorectal cancer models. Additionally, cetuximab resistance can occur due to EGFR ectodomain mutations, necessitating alternative treatment strategies.

old_summary: Rituximab is a chimaeric pan-B antibody targeting CD20, developed by Biogen (formerly Biogen Idec) (IDEC before the merger), Genentech (Roche), Roche (for Europe), and Zenyaku Kogyo (for Japan), as an anticancer agent. It depletes both normal and malignant B-cells through a complement-mediated process but does not affect precursor cells (Direct communication, IDEC, Apr 1994; Scrip, 1994, 1974, 26; Company Pipeline, Roche, 22 Apr 2015, http://www.roche.com/research_and_development/who_we_are_how_we_work/pipeline.htm; PMDA Web Page, 27 Sep 2021, Page 9, https://www.pmda.go.jp/files/000245198.pdf).
new_summary: Rituximab is a monoclonal antibody targeting CD20, used to improve prognosis in CD20-positive B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma (B-NHL). However, some patients experience disease progression or relapse post-rituximab therapy.

old_summary: Pucotenlimab (HX-008) is a recombinant humanized anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody, developed by Taizhou Hanzhong Biotechnology for the treatment of melanoma (China Clinical Trials Registry, CTR20180125, 23 Feb 2018, http://www.chinadrugtrials.org.cn/). It is also under development for breast, liver, thyroid, gastric or gastric oesophageal junction cancers and MSI-H/dMMR solid tumours (ClinicalTrials.gov, 5 Feb 2021, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04741165; ClinicalTrials.gov, 5 Oct 2020, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04574817; China Clinical Trials Registry, CTR20181881, 18 Mar 2019, http://www.chinadrugtrials.org.cn; ClinicalTrials.gov, 12 Oct 2018, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT03704246; ClinicalTrials.gov, 24 Jul 2020 & 3 Nov 2020, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04486651). It is also under development for the treatment of non-small cell lung cancer. It was previously under development for thyroid cancer (ClinicalTrials.gov, 11 Feb 2021, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04750083; Company presentation, Lepu Biopharma, 28 Apr 2021, Page 6, https://www1.hkexnews.hk/app/sehk/2021/103373/documents/sehk21042802765.pdf). It is also under development for MSI-H/dMMR metastatic colorectal cancer (China FDA, CTR20222431, 30 May 2023, http://www.chinadrugtrials.org.cn/). It is also under development for the treatment of non-small cell lung cancer and MSI-H/dMMR (Company pipeline, HanX Biopharmaceutical, 14 Nov 2022, http://www.hanxbio.com/cpgx; Company pipeline, Lepu Biopharma, 23 Aug 2023, https://www.lepubiopharma.com/technology#chanpinguanxian).
new_summary: Pucotenlimab, a PD-1 inhibitor, is being studied in combination with MRG002-HER2 ADC in patients with locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma.

old_summary: Talquetamab (JNJ-64407564; JNJ-7564; Talvey) is a humanized bispecific antibody targeting GPRC5D and CD3, developed by Johnson & Johnson (Janssen Research & Development) for the treatment of multiple myeloma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 15 Jan 2018 & 8 Nov 2019, https://clinicaltrials.gov/show/NCT03399799; Web Page, Mount Sinai, 26 Jan 2018, http://www.mountsinai.org/care/cancer/services/multiple-myeloma/clinical-trials; Company pipeline, Genmab, 18 Jan 2019, http://www.genmab.com/product-pipeline/products-in-development; Press release, Genmab, 6 Nov 2019, Page 18, https://ir.genmab.com/static-files/02eb5830-8771-43a6-a7b9-78f1b1b183ba; ClinicalTrials.gov, 20 May 2020, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04108195; Press release, Johnson & Johnson, 10 Aug 2023, https://www.janssen.com/fda-approves-talveytm-talquetamab-tgvs-first-class-bispecific-therapy-treatment-patients-heavily).
new_summary: Talquetamab is a bispecific T-cell engager targeting GPRC5D, approved in August 2023 for patients with relapsed-refractory multiple myeloma who have received at least 4 prior lines of therapy. The study evaluated its real-world safety and efficacy, showing high responses in patients previously treated with B cell maturation antigen directing therapy, although prior use of such therapies within 6 months of talquetamab was associated with inferior outcomes.

old_summary: Elranatamab (PF-06863135; Elrexfio) is a BCMA-CD3 bispecific antibody, developed by Pfizer for the treatment of multiple myeloma (ClinicalTrials.gov, 1 Sep 2017, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT03269136; Company Pipeline, Pfizer, 29 Jul 2019, Slide 11, https://www.pfizer.com/sites/default/files/product-pipeline/Pipeline_Update_29Jul2019.pdf; Qtr report, Pfizer, 2 Feb 2021, Slide 13, https://s21.q4cdn.com/317678438/files/doc_financials/2020/q4/Q4-2020-PFE-Earnings-Release.pdf; Company presentation, Pfizer, 1 Aug 2023, Slide 20, https://s28.q4cdn.com/781576035/files/doc_financials/2023/q2/Q2-2023-Earnings-Charts-FINAL.pdf).
new_summary: Elranatamab is a humanized bispecific antibody approved for the treatment of relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (RRMM) in the US, Europe, and several additional countries. It has shown efficacy in improving symptoms and overall quality of life in patients with RRMM, as demonstrated in the MagnetisMM-3 trial.

old_summary: Datopotamab deruxtecan (DS-1062; Dato-DXd) is an antibody-drug conjugate consisting of a anti-TROP2 IgG1 monoclonal antibody - datopotamab - conjugated to deruxtecan, an exatecan derivative topoisomerase I inhibitor, via a stable tetrapeptide-based cleavable linker, developed by Daiichi Sankyo for the treatment of non-small cell lung cancer, breast cancer (JP Morgan 35th Ann Healthcare Conf (San Francisco), 2017, Slide 32, http://www.daiichisankyo.com/media_investors/investor_relations/ir_calendar/files/005330/Materials.pdf; Company pipeline, AstraZeneca, 25 Apr 2024, https://www.astrazeneca.com/our-therapy-areas/pipeline.html; Press release, Daiichi Sankyo, 27 Dec 2024, https://www.daiichisankyo.com/files/news/pressrelease/pdf/202412/20241227_E.pdf; Presentation, AstraZeneca, 10 Feb 2025, Slide-8, https://www.astrazeneca.com/content/dam/az/PDF/2024/fy/Full-year-and-Q4-2024-results-presentation.pdf; WeChat, Daiichi Sankyo, 24 Jun 2025, https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/7Rb3TDfPYXfl_7S3E1xntA).
new_summary: Datopotamab deruxtecan (Dato-DXd) is a TROP2-directed antibody-drug conjugate that demonstrates remarkable antitumor activity in uterine serous carcinoma and is under investigation in pancreatic cancer. It has shown significant preclinical activity both in vitro and in vivo, with a notable survival advantage in xenograft models.

old_summary: Glofitamab (Columvi; RG-6026; RO-7082859) is an anti-CD20/CD3 bispecific antibody, developed by Roche for the treatment of B-cell malignancies. It comprises two CD20 binding Fabs (derived from the Type II CD20 IgG1 obinutuzumab), one CD3e binding Fab (fused to one of the CD20 Fabs via a short flexible linker), and an engineered, heterodimeric Fc region with completely abolished binding to FcgRs and C1q. It is also under development for the treatment of mantle cell lymphoma (FDA Orphan Drug List, 22 Feb 2022, https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/opdlisting/oopd/detailedIndex.cfm?cfgridkey=864121; 1st Qtr Rep, Roche, 2023, Slide 52, https://assets.cwp.roche.com/f/126832/x/469b6bc1c5/irp230426-a.pdf).
new_summary: Glofitamab is a CD3xCD20 bispecific antibody approved for the treatment of relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) after two or more lines of systemic therapy. It has shown high complete response rates and durable responses with manageable toxicity. In combination with GemOx, it demonstrated increased T cell infiltration, proliferation, and cytotoxic potential, resulting in greater anti-tumor activity compared to glofitamab alone or GemOx alone.

old_summary: Ivonescimab (AK-112; SMT-112) is a humanized tetravalent bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF, developed by Akeso Biopharma for the treatment of solid tumours (Company pipeline, Akeso, 27 Nov 2016 & 16 Nov 2020 & 4 Sep 2023, https://www.akesobio.com/en/rd-and-science/pipelines/; ClinicalTrials.gov, 6 Aug 2019, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04047290). It is also under development for gynaecological, ovarian cancer, small-cell lung and non-small cell lung cancers (Ann Res, Akeso, 2021, Slide 21, https://www.akesobio.com/media/1405/akesoplus2020plusarpluspresentation_ve-website2.pdf; ClinicalTrials.gov, 3 Feb 2021, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04736823; ClinicalTrials.gov, 11 Aug 2021, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04999605). It is also under development for breast cancer, colorectal cancer, oesophageal cancer, gastric cancer, head and neck cancer (HNSCC), liver cancer and renal cancer (ASCO data discussion presentation, Akeso, Jun 2021, Slide 10, 17 & 22, https://www.akesobio.com/media/1431/akeso-2021-asco-data-explanation-final-for-website.pdf). It was under development for biliary cancer pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC). It is also under development for gastro-oesophageal junction cancer (Annual Results presentation, Akeso, 31 Mar 2022, Slide 26, https://www.akesobio.com/media/1643/%E6%8C%82%E7%BD%91%E7%89%88-akeso-fy21-results331_ve-1.pdf). It is also under development for cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (ClinicalTrial.gov, 19 Dec 2024, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06567314).
new_summary: The safety and efficacy of ivonescimab in combination with chemotherapy as first-line treatment for triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC).

old_summary: Prolgolimab (BCD-100; Fortek; Caesura) is a fully human anti-PD1 monoclonal antibody, developed by Biocad for the treatment of melanoma, non-small cell lung cancer and renal cell carcinoma (Press release, Biocad, 23 Mar 2016, http://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20160323005683/en/Innovative-Russian-Medicine-Mobilizes-Human-Immune-System; Company pipeline, Biocad, 4 Apr 2016, http://biocadglobal.com/pipelines/; Russian Clinical Trial Registry, 13 Oct 2016, http://grls.rosminzdrav.ru/CiPermitionReg.aspx?DateBeg=&DateEnd=&DateInc=&NumInc=&RegNm=&Statement=&Protocol=BCD-100-1; Press release, Biocad, 20 Apr 2020, https://biocad.ru/post/minzdrav_rossii_odobril_primenenie_otechestvennogo_preparata_dlya_lecheniya_melanomy; Company Web Page, Biocad, 21 May 2021, https://biocadglobal.com/products/all?product=4; Company pipeline, Shanghai Pharmaceuticals Holding, 9 Oct 2023, https://www.sphchina.com/main_business/pharmaceutical_R&D.html; Ann Rep, Shanghai Pharmaceuticals Holding, 28 Mar 2025, Slide 17, https://www1.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/sehk/2025/0327/2025032702199.pdf).
new_summary: Prolgolimab, as part of the BCD-217 combination therapy with nurulimab, was evaluated in the OCTAVA trial. The study compared the efficacy and safety of prolgolimab+nurulimab combination therapy with prolgolimab monotherapy as a first-line treatment for patients with unresectable or metastatic melanoma. The combination therapy showed a significant improvement in progression-free survival compared to prolgolimab monotherapy.

old_summary: Epcoritamab (Epkinly; GEN-3013; DuoBody CD-3x-CD20) is a humanized IgG1 bispecific antibody targeting CD3 and CD20, developed by Genmab using its DuoBody platform for the treatment of B-cell malignancies (JP Morgan 34th Ann Healthcare Conf (San Francisco), 2016; Company Web Page, Genmab, 25 Feb 2016, http://www.genmab.com/about-us; ClinicalTrials.gov, 10 Aug 2018, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/study/NCT03625037;Press release, Genmab, 6 Nov 2019, Page 14, https://ir.genmab.com/static-files/02eb5830-8771-43a6-a7b9-78f1b1b183ba; Company pipeline, Genmab, 19 Mar 2018 & 25 Feb 2020, http://www.genmab.com/product-pipeline/products-in-development). It is also under development for the treatment of relapsed/refractory chronic lymphocytic leukaemia (ClinicalTrials.gov, 10 Nov 2020, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04623541).
new_summary: Epcoritamab is a subcutaneous CD3xCD20 bispecific antibody that has shown promising results in treating relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma and follicular lymphoma. It has demonstrated deep and durable responses with a manageable safety profile in clinical trials.

old_summary: Loncastuximab tesirine (MT-2111; ADCT-402) is a novel antibody-drug conjugate containing humanized monoclonal antibody targeting CD19 with a pyrrolobenzodiazepine warhead (SG-3199), developed by Auven Therapeutics (ADC Therapeutics) for the treatment of non-Hodgkin's lymphoma and acute lymphogenous leukaemia (Company pipeline, ADC Therapeutics, 23 Dec 2015 & 29 Jun 2020, https://adctherapeutics.com/pipeline/, Press release, ADC Therapeutics, 4 Dec 2015, http://www.adctherapeutics.com/news/2015/12/adc-therapeutics-receives-fda-clearance-to-begin-clinical-studies-for-its-second-novel-antibody-conjugate-adct-402-against-lymphomas-and-leukemia; Press release, ADC Therapeutics, 23 Apr 2021, https://ir.adctherapeutics.com/press-releases/press-release-details/2021/ADC-Therapeutics-Announces-FDA-Approval-of-ZYNLONTA-loncastuximab-tesirine-lpyl-in-Relapsed-or-Refractory-Diffuse-Large-B-Cell-Lymphoma/default.aspx; ClinicalTrials.gov, 20 Dec 2022, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT05658562).
new_summary: Loncastuximabtesirine is a CD19 antibody drug conjugate evaluated in various clinical trials for its efficacy in treating refractory or relapsed aggressive B cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma. It has shown promising results in combination with other agents, demonstrating significant response rates in patients with high-risk conditions.

old_summary: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (AZD-4552) is an antibody-drug conjugate using a novel linker-payload system comprising a potent topoisomerase I inhibitor exatecan derivative (deruxtecan) conjugated to the anti-HER2 MAb trastuzumab, developed by Daiichi Sankyo in collaboration with AstraZeneca for the treatment of cancer (ClinicalTrials.gov, 5 Oct 2015, https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/study/NCT02564900; Cancer Sci, Jul 2016, http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/27166974; Company pipeline, Daiichi, 1 May 2017, Slide 1 & 3, http://www.daiichisankyo.com/rd/pipeline/pdf/20170511_Pipeline_EN.pdf).
new_summary: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) is an antibody-drug conjugate targeting HER2, approved for treating HER2-positive and HER2-low breast cancers. It induces immunogenic cell death and immune cell activation in human breast cancer tissues.

old_summary: Cemiplimab (REGN-2810) is a fully-humanized monoclonal antibody targeting PD-1, developed by Regeneron for the treatment of cancer (Press release, Regeneron, 10 Feb 2015; Company pipeline, Regeneron, 12 Feb 2015 & 17 Oct 2015, https://www.regeneron.com/pipeline, 3rd Qtr Res, Sanofi, 29 Oct 2015, Page 27; R&D milestones presentation, Sanofi, 29 Apr 2016, Slide 1; Corporate presentation, Regeneron, 9 Feb 2017, Slide 6). It is also under development for glioblastoma (Press release, Regeneron, 8 May 2017; ClinicalTrials.gov, 20 Sep 2017 & 14 Aug 2019, https://clinicaltrials.gov/show/NCT02383212; Press release, Sanofi, 28 Sep 2018; JP Morgan 37th Ann Healthcare Conf (San Francisco), 2019, Slide 17). Sanofi has discontinued the development of the combination of isatixumab and cemiplimab for relapsed refractory multiple myeloma due to insufficient additional efficacy over isatuximab monotherapy. It was previously under development for Hodgkin's lymphoma (Press release, Sanofi, 29 Jul 2020, Page 11 & 28, https://www.sanofi.com/en/media-room/press-releases/2020/2020-07-29-15-29-13-2069605). It is also under development for Head and neck cancer (ClinicalTrials.gov, 3 March 2025, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06855212). It is also under development for the treatment of pancreas adenocarcinoma (PDAC) (ClinicalTrials.gov, 19 June 2025, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT04543071). It is also under development for the treatment of bladder cancer (ClinicalTrials.gov, 2 Sep 2025, https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06571708).
new_summary: Efficacy of cemiplimab as monotherapy or in combination with chemotherapy in Japanese patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (aNSCLC).
